div1.entryFree:
Hoise, 1) to lift, to draw up: --d sail, R3 IV, 4, 529.
2) to carry off, to make away with, to heave away (cf. Heave): there they --d us, Tp. I, 2, 148 (O. Edd. hoist). we'll h. Duke Humphrey from his seat, H6B I, 1, 169. 'tis the sport to have the enginer --d with his own petar, Hml. III, 4, 207 (O. Edd. hoist).
